html: ⚠ Figure failed to load

elements: Five black-framed camera frusta with central pinhole (aperture) and image plane, spaced evenly along a line
Five thick red arrows aligned horizontally, passing through each pinhole representing forward motion direction
Five black dots at centers of frusta indicating the camera center

interactions.label: Camera spacing
interactions.teaches: Shows how the distance between cameras affects visualization of continuous forward motion.

interactions.label: Arrow length
interactions.teaches: Represents how the speed of forward motion changes the visualization—longer arrows mean faster camera movement.

interactions.label: Number of cameras
interactions.teaches: Lets you compare few or many camera positions to see smoothness or discreteness of the forward motion path.

demo_steps.title: Single Step Forward
demo_steps.narration: Let's start by visualizing a camera at just two positions on its path forward. This makes it easy to see there is a clear direction of motion along the camera's optical axis, represented by the red arrows.

demo_steps.title: Multiple Steps Show Path
demo_steps.narration: Now, let's increase the number of camera snapshots to see several discrete steps along the forward movement. Notice how the cameras line up, all looking in the same direction, clearly illustrating a straight path forward.

demo_steps.title: Motion Speed Comparison
demo_steps.narration: Finally, let's make the movement faster by lengthening the arrows. Longer arrows suggest a higher speed of forward translation, emphasizing that a faster camera moves along the same path but covers more distance between snapshots.

interactionPlan.camera_suggestion: Start from a slight angle above and to the side, showing all frusta and arrows spaced horizontally, with enough zoom to keep the full sequence in view.
interactionPlan.notes: For clarity, ensure all frusta remain aligned and arrows always point along the optical axis. Avoid occlusion by spacing along the horizontal axis. All cameras should remain upright with identical orientation, only differing in position.

attempts.html: ⚠ Figure failed to load

discrepancies: The generated figure adds interactive sliders and step buttons which are not present in the source static image.
Arrow lengths, thickness, and proportions differ somewhat from the source; arrows in the reference are longer and more dominant.
Reference figure has six cameras; default generated has five (although this can be adjusted interactively).
Spacing and proportions of frusta/cameras and arrows do not exactly match the tidy, even spacing of the source.
No labels or text annotations are present in either the source or generated figure.
evaluation.notes: Interactive features are excellent and pedagogical, minor geometric/proportional and default element count mismatches with the source but all major elements are present and clear.
action_items: Modify default number of cameras to match the source image (six) for instant visual faithfulness.
Adjust arrow thickness and length to better match the more prominent look in the source figure.
Tune frustum and camera geometry to more closely match spatial relations and neat spacing of the original.

feedback.rationale: All conceptual elements are correct, and there are no failure modes or plan errors—requested improvements are purely about default values, geometry, and styling, which the generator can address.
action_items: Modify default number of cameras to match the source image (six) for instant visual faithfulness.
Adjust arrow thickness and length to better match the more prominent look in the source figure.
Tune frustum and camera geometry to more closely match spatial relations and neat spacing of the original.

attempts.html: ⚠ Figure failed to load

discrepancies: No labels on any part of the figure, while the source also does not include labels (so this is accurate).
Red arrows in generated figure are thicker and start at the center of the black circle, whereas in the source they slightly precede the circle.
Camera frustum geometry is represented in 3D with some additional gray center line, while the source is 2D and lacks this element.
The black pinhole is a perfect sphere in the generated figure, but a filled 2D circle in the original.
The arrow cone is more 3D and prominent in the generated version.
evaluation.notes: Faithful interactive rendering; minor stylistic differences (3D shape, gray centerline), but excellent fidelity, interactivity, and clarity.
action_items: Reduce arrow shaft thickness and position tails to begin just before each camera's pinhole to match the schematic look and alignment of the source.
Consider flattening or visually simplifying the camera frustum to appear closer to the original's 2D appearance (remove gray centerline if not present in source).
Adjust arrow and pinhole to visually match proportions and style of the source illustration.

feedback.rationale: The feedback concerns only minor stylistic and visual adjustments, and all scores are high with no conceptual or usability failures—further iteration is unlikely to yield substantial improvement.
